Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 028 [SB Nergal and Ereškigal][via cams/gkab]

o i 1'o 1'

[... UN]-MEŠ lud-lul

(o i 1') Let me praise [...] of the people.

(o i 2') [...] ... continuously

(o i 4') [...] ... continuously

(o i 5') [...] ...

[... gi]-mil dum-qi₂

(o i 6') [...] a recompense

o i 7'7'

[... i]-na diš-tar-MEŠ ka-li-šin

(o i 7') [...] among all the goddesses

o i 8'8'

[... re]-me-ni-it

(o i 8') [...] she is merciful

o i 9'9'

[...] ⸢me₂⸣-e-ši

(o i 9') [...] forgive [...]

o i 10'10'

[... ar]-nu-u₂-na

(o i 10') [...] our sins

[...]-MEŠ-u₂

(o i 11') [...] ...

(o i 12') [...] ...

(o i 13') [...] ...

(o i 14') [...] ...

(16 lines missing)
o i 31'31'

[...] ka-a-ši

(o i 31') [...] to you

o i 32'32'

[...] ša₂-ma-mi

(o i 32') [...] of heavens

o i 33'33'

[... ka-ša₂]-di-šu

(o i 33') [...] (of) his arrival

o i 34'34'

[...] ⸢pi⸣-[ta-an]-ni KA₂

(o i 34') [...] "Open the gate to me!"

o i 35'35'

[... e-ru]-um-ma lik-⸢ru⸣-[bak-ka] KA₂

(o i 35') "Enter [...] and may the gate bless you!"

o i 36'36'

[...] u₂-še-ri-⸢ba⸣ [... d]⸢ka₃⸣-ka₃

(o i 36') He brought [...] Kakka in [...],

o i 37'37'

[...] KA₂ u₂-še-ri-⸢ba⸣ [x] dka₃-ka₃

(o i 37') He brought [...] Kakka in through the [...] gate,

o i 38'38'

[3]ša₂ KA₂ u₂-še-ri-ba [x] dka₃-ka₃

(o i 38') He brought [...] Kakka in through the third gate,

o i 39'39'

⸢4⸣u ⸢KA₂⸣ u₂-še-ri-⸢ba DINGIR⸣ dka₃-⸢ka₃⸣

(o i 39') He brought the god Kakka in through the fourth gate,

o i 40'40'

5ša₂ ⸢KA₂⸣ u₂-še-⸢ri⸣-[ba] ⸢DINGIR⸣ dka₃-⸢ka₃⸣

(o i 40') He brought the god Kakka in through the fifth gate,

o i 41'41'

6ša₂ KA₂ u₂-⸢še⸣-[ri]-⸢ba DINGIR⸣ dka₃-⸢ka₃⸣

(o i 41') He brought the god Kakka in through the sixth gate,

o i 42'42'

7u KA₂ u₂-⸢še⸣-[ri]-⸢ba DINGIR⸣ dka₃-ka₃

(o i 42') He brought the god Kakka in through the seventh gate.

o i 43'43'

i-ru-um-ma ana ⸢pal⸣-ki-i ki-sal-li-ša₂

(o i 43') He then entered into her wide court, he knelt (and) kissed the ground in front of her; he straightens, stands [...] speaks to her:

o i 44'44'

ik-mi-si ⸢iš⸣-[ši-iq] ⸢qaq⸣-qa-ru maḫ-ri-ša₂

o i 45'45'

i-šir₃ i-za-⸢zi⸣ [...] i-zak-kar-ša₂

o i 46'46'

da-nu₃ AD-[ki] ⸢iš-pur-ra-an-ni⸣ [...]

(o i 46') "Anu, your father, sent me [...] saying:

o i 47'47'

um-ma at-⸢ti-ma⸣ ul ša₂ e-li-[i]

(o i 47') 'As for you, you are not to come up. During your year, you will not come up in front of us, and as for us, we are not to go down. During our month, we will not come down in front of you.

o i 48'48'

ina MU.AN.⸢NA-ki⸣-ma ul te-li-i ana maḫ-ri-ni

o i 49'49'

u ni-nu-⸢ma⸣ ul ša₂ a-ra-di

o i 50'50'

ina ITIi-ni-ma ul nu-ur-rad ana maḫ-⸢ri-ki⸣

o i 51'51'

lu₂DUMU šip-ri-ki lil-li-kam₂-ma

(o i 51') 'Let your messenger come, let him clear away the offering table, let him receive your presents. Whatever I give him, let him deliver ... safely to you'."

o i 52'52'

gišBANŠUR li-ip-ṭur qiš-ta-a-⸢ka⸣ lim-ḫur

o i 53'53'

mim-mu-u₂ a-nam-din-aš₂-šu₂ lu-šal-li-ma-kum₂ x x ka-ši

o i 54'54'

⸢d⸣EREŠ.KI.GAL pa-aš₂-šu₂ i-pu-uš-⸢ma DU₁₁⸣.GA ana ⸢dka₃⸣-[ka₃] a-mat MU

(o i 54') Ereškigal opened her (lit. his) mouth to speak; she said (these) words to Kakka:

o i 55'55'

lu₂DUMU šip-ri ša₂ da-nu₃ AD-ni ša₂ tal-li-⸢ka-na⸣-a-ši

(o i 55') "Messenger of Anu, our father, who has come to us, may health be with Anu, Ellil and Ea, the Great Gods. May health be with Nammu and the pure Nanše. May health be with the husband of the lady of the heavens. May health be with Ninurta the mightiest in the land."

o i 56'56'

lu-u₂ šul-mu ana da-nu₃ dEN.LIL₂ u de₂-a DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ

o i 57'57'

lu-u₂ šul-mu ana dNAMMA u d⸢na⸣-aš₂ <<d>>KU₃.GA

o i 58'58'

lu-u₂ šul-mu ana mu-ti ⸢be⸣-let ša₂-ma-mi

o i 59'59'

lu-u₂ šul-mu ana dNIN.URTA ⸢dan?-dan?-nu?⸣ ina KUR

o i 60'60'

dka₃-ka₃ ⸢pa⸣-[šu₂] i-pu-uš-ma ⸢DU₁₁⸣.GA ana dEREŠ.KI.GAL a-[mat] MUar₂

(o i 60') Kakka opened his mouth to speak; he said (these) words to Ereškigal:

o i 61'61'

šul-mu ana da-nu₃ dEN.LIL₂ u de₂-a DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ

(o i 61') "Health is with Anu, Ellil and Ea the great gods. Health is with Nammu and the pure Nanše. Health is [...] the lady of the heavens. Health is with Ninurta, the mightiest in the land."

o i 62'62'

šul-mu ana d⸢NAMMA⸣ u d⸢na⸣-aš₂ KU₃.GA

o i 63'63'

šul-⸢mu⸣ [... be]-let ša₂-ma-mi

o i 64'64'

⸢šul⸣-[mu] [ana] ⸢dNIN.URTA dan-dan-nu⸣ ina KUR

o i 65'65'

[d]⸢ka₃⸣-ka₃ <KA> DU₃-<<šu₂>>⸢uš⸣-[ma] ⸢DU₁₁.GA⸣ ana dEREŠ.KI.GAL a-[mat] MUar₂

(o i 65') Kakka opened his mouth to speak; he said (these) words to Ereškigal:

o i 66'66'

[x x] x a-na ka-a-⸢ši⸣ lu-u₂ ša₂-lim-tu₄

(o i 66') "[...] ... may (all) be well with you."

o i 67'67'

[...] DU₃uš!(.ŠU₂) KA-šu₂-ma DU₁₁.⸢GA⸣ ana dNAM.TAR SUKKAL-šu₂ a-mat MUar₂

(o i 67') [...] opened his mouth to speak; she said (these) words to Namtar, her (lit. his) minister:

o i 68'68'

[d]⸢NAM⸣.[TAR] ⸢SUKKAL⸣ lu-uš-pur-⸢ka⸣ [x x x x d]⸢a-nu₃⸣ AD-nu

(o i 68') "Namtar, my minister, let me send you to [...] Anu, our father. Namtar, go up the long [...], clear away the offering table [...], whatever Anu [...]."

o i 69'69'

⸢d⸣NAM.TAR e-⸢li-i ar₂-kat⸣ [...]

o i 70'70'

⸢giš⸣BANŠUR pu-ṭur [...]

o i 71'71'

⸢mim⸣-mu-u₂ d[a-nu₃] NA [...]

o i 72'72'

⸢d⸣[NAM.TAR] [...]

(o i 72') Namtar [...]

(o ii 13) [...] ... the way ... [...]

(o ii 14) His [...] together ... [...]

o ii 1515

[...] x be-el ši-ma-a-[ti]

(o ii 15) [...] ... lord of destinies

o ii 1616

[...] u₂-kal-la par-ṣi u₂-kal-la par-ṣi [x x]

(o ii 16) [...] he holds the divine powers, he holds the divine powers [...]

o ii 1717

[...]-MEŠ a-ši-bu-ut qe₂-reb der-[kal-li]

(o ii 17) "[...] who dwell inside the Underworld, why don't you kneel in front of him?

o ii 1818

⸢ina⸣ pa-ni-šu₂ am-mi₃-ni la ta-⸢kam⸣-[mis]

o ii 1919

[x] x IGI-MIN-<<MEŠ>>-ia aṣ-ṣa-nap-rak-kam₂-ma

(o ii 19) [...] I keep squinting my eyes at you but you have turned into an ignoramus and [...] ... your eyes look at the very ground."

o ii 2020

[te]-⸢er⸣-ra-a-ta a-na la mu-de-e-⸢ma⸣

o ii 2121

[x x] x TA qaq-qa-<<ru>>-ram-ma i-na!-ṭal ⸢e⸣-[na]-⸢ka⸣

[...] x x x x [...] x

(o ii 22) [...] ... [...] ...

(o ii 23) [...]

(o ii 24) [...]

(o ii 25) [...] ...

(o ii 26) [...] ...

o ii 2727

[...] x [...]

(o ii 27) [...] ... [...]

o ii 2828

[... lu]-ut-be₂ [x]

(o ii 28) [...] let me arise [...]

o ii 2929

[...] x taq-bi

(o ii 29) [...] ... you said

o ii 3030

[x x] ṢI ma-la DINGIR x ana? 2-šu₂ eṣ-ṣip-šu₂

(o ii 30) [...] ... as much as the god ..., I will twine it double

o ii 3131

[d]e₂-a an-ni-tu₂ ina še-me-šu₂ zik-ra it-ta-mi ana ŠA₃-šu₂

(o ii 31) On hearing this speech, Ea said to himself: "Let me work on (that) and ... to send."

o ii 3232

[lu-uš]-⸢te⸣-pi-iš-ma x QA RA A GA TI ša₂-pa!-a-ra

o ii 3333

⸢d⸣e₂-a pa-a-šu₂ i-pu-uš-ma DU₁₁.GA ana dNERGALₓ(U.GUR) a-mat MUar₂

(o ii 33) Ea opened his mouth to speak; he said to Nergal:

o ii 3434

DUMU ḫar-ra-ni te-ri-šu₂ tal-lak na-aš-[par₂]-ta-ka GIR₂ SUḪ <ŠU>-MIN-ka

(o ii 34) "Traveller, do you wish to go on your mission? Draw the sword in your hand, go down to the forest [...] of the mēsu-trees. Cut down the mēsu-tree, the white cedar and the supālu-juniper, break off (the branches) of the kanakātu-tree and the sibbirru-tree."

o ii 3535

e-rid a-⸢na⸣ gišTIR [...] ša₂ ⸢giš⸣MES

o ii 3636

i-kis gišMES giš⸢ti!⸣-[ʾa-a-lu u] gišsu-pa-a-lu

o ii 3737

ḫu-uṣ-ṣib giš⸢ka⸣-[na-ka-a-tu₄ u] gišsi-ib-bir-ri

o ii 3838

[d]⸢NERGALₓ(U.GUR)⸣ an-ni!(NI₃-)[tu₄ ... še-me]-⸢e⸣-šu x

(o ii 38) Nergal, (on) hearing this ... [...] ... [...] supālu-juniper, he broke off (the branches of) the [...] and (of) the sibbirru-tree, and thereafter ... [...] for Anu and Ningišzida.

o ii 3939

[...] x [... giš]⸢su⸣-pa-a-⸢lu!⸣

o ii 4040

u₂!-ḫa!-aṣ!-[ṣib ...] gišsi-ib-bir-ra

o ii 4141

⸢ar₂-ki-iš⸣-ma AB [... da]-nu₃ u dNIN.GIŠ.ZI.DA

o ii 4242

me₂-eḫ-rat na₄ZA.[GIN₃ ...]-šu₂ u₂-⸢taq-qi₂⸣

(o ii 42) In the likeness of lapis-lazuli, he painted his [...],

o ii 4343

me₂-eḫ-rat KU₃.SI₂₂ IM [...] u₂-taq-qi₂

(o ii 43) In the likeness of gold, he painted ... [...],

o ii 4444

me₂-eḫ-rat na₄ZA.GIN₃ [...] u₂-taq-qi₂

(o ii 44) In the likeness of lapis-lazuli, he painted [...].

o ii 4545

ep-še-ti qu-ut-ta-ti gišGU.[ZA] ⸢ka⸣-la-mu!(GAR)

(o ii 45) The work completed, (that was) all of the throne!

o ii 4646

u ša₂-a-šu₂ is-si-ma i-šak-ka-an-aš₂-šu₂ [...] ⸢ṭe₃-e⸣-ma

(o ii 46) And then, he (Ea) called out to him (Nergal) and gave him [...] instructions: "Traveller, do you wish to go? Whatever instructions ... [...] in (your) heart [...].

o ii 4747

DUMU ḫar-ra-ni [te]-ri-šu₂ ⸢tal-lak⸣

o ii 4848

mim-mu-u₂ UR₅.UŠ₂-MEŠ ⸢U₂?⸣ [...] A ina lib KUR [...]

o ii 4949

ul-tu₂ ul-la-nu-um-ma giš⸢GU.ZA na⸣-šu₂-nik-ka

(o ii 49) "As soon as they offer you a chair: do not look at it and do not sit on it.

o ii 5050

e ta-mir-ma e tu-šib ina muḫ-ḫi

o ii 5151

lu₂⸢MUḪALDIM⸣ NINDA-ḪI.A na-ši-[ka e] ⸢ta-mir-ma⸣ NINDA-ḪI.<A>-šu₂ ul ⸢tak₂-kul⸣

(o ii 51) "(When) the cook offers you bread: do not look at his bread, you must not eat (it).

o ii 5252

lu₂GIR₂.LA₂ UZU na-ši-[ka e ta]-⸢mir⸣-ma UZU-šu₂ ul KI.MIN<(tak₂-kul)>

(o ii 52) "(When) the butcher offers you meat: do not look at his meat, ditto (you must not eat it).

o ii 5353

lu₂⸢LUNGA₂ KAŠ⸣-MEŠ ⸢na-ši-ka e ta-mir⸣-ma KAŠ-šu₂ ⸢ul⸣ [taš]-ti

(o ii 53) "(When) the brewer offers you beer: do not look at his beer, you must not drink it.

o ii 5454

me-si-it GIR₃-MIN na-ši-ka e ta-mir-ma GIR₃-MIN-ka e tam-si

(o ii 54) "(When) he offers you water for washing (your) feet: do not look at it, do not wash your feet.

o ii 5555

⸢ši⸣-i a-na nar-ma-ki i-ru-um-ma

(o ii 55) "(When) she enters the bath and the garment [...] is put on ... she will let you see her body. As for you, [...] man or woman, do not ...."

o ii 5656

[lu]-ub-bu-ši [x] MU ŠU₂ il-la-bi-iš!?

o ii 5757

E? I? ⸢zu-mur⸣-ša₂ uš-ta-bar-rak-ka

o ii 5858

at-⸢ta⸣ [x] ⸢zi⸣-kar u sin-niš ⸢e? ta⸣-x-[x]-x-ka

o ii 5959

dNERGALₓ(U.GUR) [...]

(o ii 59) Nergal [...]

(o iii 19) "[...] let me identify him

o iii 2020

[...] ŠU₂ ina ka-mi-i KA₂

(o iii 20) "[...] ... at the outer gate.

o iii 2121

⸢ṭe₃?⸣-[me] ⸢A?⸣ x x lu-tir ana be-li₂-ia

(o iii 21) "Let me bring back instructions ... to my lady (lit. lord)."

o iii 2222

dNAM.TAR il-lik-⸢ma⸣ [ina] ⸢ṣi⸣-li gišIG ip-pa-la-šu₂ dER₃.RA

(o iii 22) Namtar went and, in the shadow of the door, gazed at Erra.

o iii 2323

ša₂ dNAM.TAR ⸢GIM⸣ ni?-⸢kis⸣ gišŠINIG ʾi-ir-qu pa-nu-uš

(o iii 23) Namtar's face became yellow-green like a tamarisk cutting, his lips become black like the lip of a kunninu-basin.

o iii 2424

GIM šap-ti ku-[ni]-⸢ni? iṣ-li⸣-ma šap-ta-šu₂

o iii 2525

dNAM.TAR il-lik-ma ana be-[el-ti]-⸢šu₂⸣ a-mat MUar₂

(o iii 25) Namtar went and said (these) words to his lady:

o iii 2626

be-el-ti ul-tu₂ [...] ⸢AD⸣-ki taš-pu-ri-ni-ma

(o iii 26) "My lady, from [...] you sent me to your father and into the court [...] (at) my entrance, they were kneeling, they were humble [...] they were kneeling."

o iii 5757

e ta-mir-ma ⸢ul⸣ [...]

(o iii 57) Do not look at, you must not [...]

o iii 5858

lu₂MUḪALDIM NINDA-ḪI.A na-šu-niš-[šu e ta-mir]-⸢ma⸣ [NINDA]-ḪI.A ⸢ul e⸣-[kul]

(o iii 58) The cook offers him bread: "do not look at the bread"; he did not eat (it).

o iii 5959

[lu₂]⸢GIR₂.LA₂ UZU⸣ na-šu-niš-šu e ta-mir-ma UZU-šu₂ ⸢KI.MIN<(ul)> e⸣-[kul]

(r i 1') [...] of ... [...] ...

r i 2'2'

[... di]-ma-tu [i-za]-⸢an⸣-nu-un

(r i 2') [...] tears rain; her tears flow (all) over her cheeks:

r i 3'3'

⸢UGU du-ur₂ ap⸣-pi-ša₂ il-la-[ka] di-ma-⸢sa₃?⸣

r i 4'4'

⸢d⸣ER₃.RA ḫa-me-ru la-le-e-a

(r i 4') "Erra, (my) husband, (full of) sexual charms, I am not sated with his sexual charms, (yet) he goes off me. Erra, (my) husband, (full of) sexual charms, I am not sated with his sexual charms, (yet) he goes off me."

r i 5'5'

ul aš₂-ba-a la-la-šu₂ ⸢it⸣-ta-tal-kan!(I-)ni

r i 6'6'

⸢d⸣ER₃.RA ḫa-me-⸢ru⸣ la-le!(e-)e!(LI-)a

r i 7'7'

ul aš₂-ba-a la-la-šu₂ ⸢it⸣-ta-tal-kan!(I-)ni

r i 8'8'

⸢dNAM⸣.TAR pa-a-šu₂ DU₃!(DU₁₁-)ma DU₁₁.GA ana dEREŠ.KI.GAL a-mat MUar₂

(r i 8') Namtar opened his mouth to speak; he said to Ereškigal:

r i 9'9'

[x x] x ŠU x x [x] x DINGIR ša₂-a-ša₂ lu ṣab-tak-ma

(r i 9') "[...] ... [...] ... indeed I wish to seize that god; let me take him to you."

r i 10'10'

[...] ⸢lul!⸣-qa-ak-ki ka-a-ši

Column ii
r iir ii  (3 lines missing)
r ii 44

[...] ⸢d⸣a-nu₃ d⸢EN⸣.LIL₂ ⸢u de₂⸣-a x x x x

(r ii 4) [...] (to) Anu, Ellil and Ea ..., saying:

r ii 55

[um]-⸢ma ul⸣-tu₂ ṣe-eḫ-ra-ku-ma DUMU.MUNUS-<<MEŠ>>-ku

(r ii 5) "Since I was small, since I was a young girl, I have not known the playing of maidens, I have not known the gamboling of young girls.

r ii 66

⸢ul⸣ i-⸢de⸣ me₂-lu-lu ša₂ KI.SIKIL-MEŠ

r ii 77

⸢ul⸣ i-[de] ⸢da-ka⸣-ka ša₂ ṣe-ḫe-ra-a-ti

r ii 88

[...] taš-[pu]-⸢ra⸣-na-šu!(NIŠ-)ma ur-ta-ḫa-ni-ma li-ta-til KI-ia₂

(r ii 8) "[...] You have sent him to me and he indeed had sexual intercourse with me. Let him lie down with me. Do send him to me. Let him be my husband, let him spend the night with me. I am indeed the impure one, I am not pure; I cannot render the judgement of the Great Gods.

r ii 99

AN [... šup]-⸢pu-ra⸣-na-šu-ma lu ḫa-me-ri li-bit KI-ia₂

r ii 1010

mu-⸢suk-ka⸣-[ku]-ma ul e-bek-<ma> ul a-da-ni di-ni ša₂ DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ

r ii 1111

DINGIR-⸢MEŠ⸣ GAL-MEŠ a-ši-bu-ut qe₂-reb der-kal-la

(r ii 11) "Great Gods, who dwell inside the Underworld, if you do not send that god, in accordance with [...] and the Great Underworld, I will raise the dead and make them devour the living, I will make the dead more numerous than the living!"

r ii 1212

šum-[ma] DINGIR ⸢ša₂⸣-[a-šu₂ la] ⸢tal⸣-tap-ra-⸢šu⸣

r ii 1313

ki-[i ...] u KIti₃ ra-⸢bi-tu₂⸣

r ii 1414

u₂-[šel]el-ma UŠ₂-MEŠ GU₇-MEŠ bal-ṭu-ti

r ii 1515

el bal-ṭu-ti u-šam-ad UŠ₂-MEŠ

r ii 1616

e-la-a dNAM.TAR ⸢ar₂-kat⸣ sisim-me-lat ša₂-⸢ma⸣-[mi]

(r ii 16) Namtar climbed up the long stairways of the heavens.

r ii 1717

ana KA₂ da-nu₃ dEN.LIL₂ u de₂-a ina ka-ša₂-⸢di⸣

(r ii 17) At his arrival at the gate of Anu, Ellil and Ea, Anu, Ellil and Ea saw him and (asked): "Why do you come, Namtar?"

r ii 1818

[da]-nu₃ dEN.LIL₂ u de₂-a e-mu-ru-šu-ma

r ii 1919

[mi]-⸢na⸣-a tal-lak dNAM.TAR

r ii 2020

[DUMU].⸢MUNUS⸣-ku-nu iš-pur-an-ni

(r ii 20) "Your daughter sent me, saying: 'Since I was small, (since) I was a young girl, I have not known the playing of maidens, I have not known the gamboling of young girls.

r ii 2121

um-ma ul-tu₂ ⸢ṣe⸣-eḫ-ra-ku-ma DUMU.MUNUS-ku

r ii 2222

ul i-de me₂-lu-lu ša₂ KI.SIKIL-MEŠ

r ii 2323

ul i-de ⸢da-ka⸣-ka ša₂ ṣe-ḫe!(EḪ-)ra-a-ti

r ii 2424

(r ii 24) 'That god, whom you sent, had indeed sexual intercourse with me. Let him lie down [...]. Send that god to me. Let him be my husband, let him spent the night with me [...]. I am indeed the impure one, I am not pure; I will not render the judgement [...].

r ii 2525

r ii 2828

r ii 3939

(r ii 39) Namtar came (back) to his Lady and said (these) words:

r ii 4040

be-el-ti [...] da-nu₃ AD-ki ša₂ taš-pu-ri-ni-ma

(r ii 40) "My Lady [...] Anu, your father, to whom you sent me, my Lady, [...] he is bald, he is squinting, he is crippled ... sitting in the assembly of all the gods."

r ii 4141

be-el-ti [... qu]-⸢bu⸣-ḫu ṣu-un-dur

r ii 4242

pu-us-⸢sul⸣ x x a-šib ⸢pu⸣-ḫur kal₃ DINGIR-MEŠ-ma

r ii 4343

a-lik DINGIR ša₂-a-šu₂ ṣab-ta-šum-ma ⸢le-qa⸣-a ⸢ia-ši⸣

(r ii 43) "Go, seize that god and take (him) to me! Ea, his father, has sprinkled him with water of the source and he is bald, he is squinting, he is crippled [...] the assembly of all gods."

KA₂ ša₂ dEREŠ.[KI.GAL] ina ⸢ka⸣-[ša₂]-di

r iii 2222

lu₂I₃.DU₈ pi-⸢ta⸣-[an-ni] KA₂

(r iii 22) "Gatekeeper, open the gate to me!"

lu₂I₃.DU₈ ša₂ KA₂ [...] KA₂ i-lul-šu-ma

ana ti-[ru-ub-ti] ul i-din

(r iii 26) the 3rd one [...] ... ditto (hung up his ... (at) the gate and did not grant (him) entrance),

r iii 2727

⸢4⸣[u₂ ...] KI.MIN<([...])> KA₂ i-lul-šu-ma ana ti-[ru-ub-ti] ul i-din

(r iii 27) the 4th one [...] ditto (hung up his ... (at) the gate and did not grant (him) entrance),

r iii 2828

⸢5⸣[ša₂ ...] ŠU₂ KI.MIN<([...])> KA₂ i-lul-šu-ma ana ti-[ru-ub-ti] ul i-din

(r iii 28) the 5th one [...] ... ditto, (hung up his ... (at) the gate and did not grant (him) entrance),

r iii 2929

[...] ŠU₂ KI.MIN<([...])> KA₂ i-lul-šu-ma ana ti-[ru-ub-ti] ul i-din

(r iii 29) [...] ... ditto (hung up his ... (at) the gate and did not grant (him) entrance),

r iii 3030

[...] ŠU₂ KI.MIN<([...])> KA₂ i-lul-šu-ma ana ti-[ru-ub-ti] ul i-din

(r iii 30) [...] ... ditto (hung up his ... (at) the gate and did not grant (him) entrance).

r iii 3131

i-⸢ru-um⸣-ma ana pal-ki-i ki-sal-li-ša₂

(r iii 31) He entered into her wide court, approached her and laughed. He seized her by her headdress. [...] from the top [...] .... He seized her by her locks.

r iii 3232

iʾ-i-⸢ir⸣-ši-ma i-ṣi-iḫ

r iii 3333

iṣ-bat-si-ma i-na up-ri-i-ša!(ŠI)

r iii 3434

ul-tu₂ ⸢UGU⸣ [...] x U₄ [...] IGI

r iii 3535

iṣ-bat-si-ma [... ab]-⸢bu⸣-ut-ti-ša₂

r iii 3636

⸢ma-la⸣ ra-ʾi-⸢i⸣-[mu]-⸢ti⸣-šu ša₂ ⸢lib₃!?⸣-[bi]-⸢šu₂⸣

(r iii 36) As much as the love of his heart, both the brother and the sister are embracing. Passionately indeed, they have entered into the bed.

r iii 3737

⸢in⸣-na-an-ad-ru-ma ŠEŠ-MEŠ ki-lal-⸢la-an⸣

r iii 3838

⸢ana gišma⸣-a-a-li šit-mu-riš i-ter-⸢bu⸣-ma

r iii 3939

1en u₄-ma 2a u₄-ma ⸢ṣal-lu₄-ma⸣ šar-rarat  ;

(r iii 39) The 1st day, the 2nd day, they lay, queen Ereškigal and Erra;

r iii 4040

d⸢EREŠ⸣.[KI.GAL u dER₃].RA

r iii 4141

3ša₂ u₄-ma [...] KI.MIN<(⸢ṣal-lu₄-ma⸣)> šar-rarat d⸢EREŠ⸣.[KI.GAL u dER₃].RA

(r iii 41) the 3rd day [...] ditto (they lay, queen Ereškigal and Erra);

r iii 4242

5ša₂ ⸢u₄-ma⸣ [...] KI.MIN<(⸢ṣal-lu₄-ma⸣)> šar-rarat d⸢EREŠ⸣.[KI.GAL u dER₃].RA

(r iii 42) the 5th day [...] ditto (they lay, queen Ereškigal and Erra);

r iii 4343

⸢6⸣ša₂ u₄-⸢ma⸣ [...] KI.MIN<(⸢ṣal-lu₄-ma⸣)> šar-rarat d⸢EREŠ⸣.[KI.GAL u dER₃].RA

(r iii 43) the 6th day [...] ditto (they lay, queen Ereškigal and Erra);

r iii 4444

[...] KI.MIN<(⸢ṣal-lu₄-ma⸣)> šar-rarat d⸢EREŠ⸣.[KI.GAL u dER₃].RA

(r iii 44) [...] ditto (they lay, queen Ereškigal and Erra).

r iii 4545

[...] ka-ša-di-i

(r iii 45) [...] my arrival.

r iii 4646

[...] DU₁₁.GA

(r iii 46) [...] speaks

r iii 4747

[...] a-mat MUar₂

(r iii 47) [...] says (these) words: "[...] let me send you [...] Ereškigal, who dwells inside the Underworld."

r iii 4848

[... lu]-uš-pur-ka

r iii 4949

[... dEREŠ.KI].⸢GAL⸣ a-ši-bat qe₂-reb

r iii 5050

[...] ⸢d⸣er-kal-la

r iii 5151

[...] x ša₂ aš₂-<<šu₂>>-pu-rak-ki

(r iii 51) [...] whom I sent to you

r iii 5252

[...]-ME da-ru-u-tu₂

(r iii 52) [...] eternal

r iii 5353

[...] e-la-a-ti

(r iii 53) [...] the upper world

r iii 5454

[... šap]-la-a-ti

(r iii 54) [...] the netherworld

r iii 5555

[...] I MA

(r iii 55) [...] ...

r iii 5656

[...] x

(r iii 56) [...] ...

(12 lines missing)
